endedAt.description: The date and time the operation completed in the `SUCCEEDED`, `FAILED`, or `CANCELLED` states.

debugId.description: Unique identifier for the instance of this error

timestamp.description: The date and time the error message was created.

id.description: The primary identifier for the resource.

message.description: The log message describing the progress update.

timestamp.description: The date and time the log messag was created.

progressPercent.description: Percent progress of the operation as an integer value of 0 to 100.

results.description: List of references to resources (other than the source resource) which were created, updated, or deleted during the operation.

name.description: The name of the effect resource.

sourceResourceUri.description: The URI reference to the resource or resource collection that initiated the operation

startedAt.description: The date and time the operation entered the `RUNNING` state.

items.description: The email address of the recipients of the report.

subject.description: The subject line of the email.

format.description: The file type of the report.

total.description: Total number of items in the collection that match the filter query, if one was provided in the request.

description.description: A short summary of the report.

columns.description: An array containing the supported columns.

dataType.description: The classification of data accepted by the column.

name.description: The internal name of the column.

info.description: The HPE GreenLake for Reporting service provides a collection of RESTful APIs for generating reports, retrieving supported columns and filters, monitoring asynchronous operations, and querying the status of reports.
info.title: HPE GreenLake for Reporting

get.description:
Retrieve information about asynchronous operations within the reporting service. Provide the unique identifier of the operation in the parameter to monitor the progress of asynchronous tasks.  This API returns the following attributes:

  - `status`—Indicates the current status of the asynchronous task.
  - `startedAt`—Specifies the timestamp when the operation was initiated.
  - `endedAt`—Indicates the timestamp when the operation was completed.
  - `logMessages`—Provides a list of progress updates for the asynchronous operation.
  - `progressPercent`—Represents the progress of the operation as a percentage value ranging from 0 to 100.
  - `sourceResourceUri`—References the source URI that triggered the asynchronous task.
  - `results`—The results array provides a link to /reporting/v1/statuses/{id} to get the complete details of the report status.

parameters.description: The unique identifier returned by an asynchronous API call.
parameters.name: id

example.message: Not authorized to get async operation details
Incorrect permission to retrieve report of id = "1fa85f64-5434-9980-b3fc-3c963f44fgh9"
Invalid id: 4ca85f64-9878-1234-b3fc-2c963f66afy7
Failed to retrieve async operation details

post.description:
Use this API to asynchronously generate reports across supported report types. After you submit your report generation requests, they are processed in the background. Once complete, you receive a unique URI in the response header.

**NOTE:** You need to specify the columns you want to return in the report, and apply filter criteria to refine the data returned. To find out what columns and filter criteria are available, call the Report Exports Metadata API.

Location.description: URL where the generated resource can be accessed

example.message: Invalid column name "SUBSCRIPTION_KEY1"
Not authorized to initiate report export
Incorrect permissions to initiate report export for report type "subscriptions"
Invalid datasource "subscription1"
Failed to initiate report exports

get.description:
This API is a support tool that assists with generating a report. Use it to find the supported columns, filter criteria, and values for a report type.  In a response, the API returns:

  - `columns`—An array containing the supported columns.
  - `filterCriteria`—An array comprising of filter names and their corresponding data types.
  - `supportedOperators`—A collection of supported operators assisting you in selecting the correct operator to use in a filter attribute. The following operators are supported:
    - `EQ`—Checks if a field is equal to a value.
    - `NE`—Checks if a field is not equal to a value.
    - `LT`—Checks if a field is less than a value.
    - `LE`—Checks if a field is less than or equal to a value.
    - `GT`—Checks if a field is greater than a value.
    - `GE`—Checks if a field is greater than or equals to a value.
    - `IN`—Checks if a value is in a list.

parameters.description:
Limit the resources operated on by an endpoint or when used with a multiple-GET endpoint,
return only the subset of resources that match the filter. The filter grammar is a subset
of OData 4.0.

**NOTE:** The filter query parameter must use [URL encoding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/URL_encoding).
Most clients do this automatically with inputs provided to them specifically as query parameters. Encoding must be done manually for any query parameters provided as part of the URL.  
The reserved characters `!` `#` `$` `&` `'` `(` `)` `*` `+` `,` `/` `:` `;` `=` `?` `@` `[` `]` must be encoded with percent encoded equivalents.

For example: the value `P06760-B21+2M212504P8` must be encoded as `P06760-B21%2B2M212504P8` when it is used in a query parameter.

| CLASS     |  EXAMPLES                                          |
|-----------|----------------------------------------------------|
| Types     | integer, decimal, timestamp, string, boolean, null |
| Operations| eq, ne, gt, ge, lt, le, in                         |
| Logic     | and, or, not                                       |

equals.description:
Return activities where a property equals a value.
\<property> eq \<value>
equals.value: name ne Subscriptions and group eq Device inventory

parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: The select query parameter is used to limit the properties returned for a resource. The value of the select query parameter is a comma-separated list of properties.

equals.description: Return activities where a property equals a value.

parameters.name: select

parameters.description: The order in which to return the resources in the collection. The value of the sort query parameter is a comma separated list of sort expressions. Each sort expression is a property name optionally followed by a direction indicator asc (ascending) or desc (descending). The first sort expression in the list defines the primary sort order, the second defines the secondary sort order, and so on. If a direction indicator is omitted, the default direction is ascending.

name-createdAt-desc.description: Order resources ascending by name and then by descending by createdAt
name-createdAt-desc.summary: Ascending by name and then by descending by createdAt

parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: The maximum number of reports to return.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Zero-based resource offset to start the response from.
parameters.name: offset

example.message: Invalid attribute: type1
Not authorized to retrieve report export metadata
Incorrect permission to retrieve report export metadata for report type "subscriptions"
Invalid type "subscription1"
Failed to retrieve report exports metadata

get.description: This API is designed to fetch the status of all reports for a specific workspace. Only reports belonging to the workspace ID and username are returned. This API supports pagination, allowing you to use offset and limit parameters.

equals.description:
Return results where a property equals a value.
\<property> eq \<value>

For example, the value `P06760-B21+2M212504P8` must be encoded as `P06760-B21%2B2M212504P8` when it is used in a query parameter.

stage.description: The current stage in the report generation process.

startTime.description: The date and time the report request was initiated.

status.description: The current status of the report generation stage, indicating whether the report stage is, for example, in progress or completed.

statusTimestamp.description: The date and time the report status last changed.

userName.description: The username of whoever created the report.

limit.description: The maximum number of reports to return.
